scenario: Stellar Manufacturer runs a Camunda 8.8 quality inspection workflow on 8000 records per day. An ad-hoc sub-process runs varied inspection steps; each inner flow writes a local result variable, and every inner result must be appended to one list that is propagated to the parent scope when the sub-process completes.
question: Which configuration on the ad-hoc sub-process collects each inner flow's result into one list propagated to the parent scope?

options.text: Define an input variable mapping that copies result into a parent-scoped variable each time an inner flow completes inside the ad-hoc sub-process
Define a completionCondition with an append() call that pushes each inner flow's result onto a parent-scoped variable as inner flows finish one by one
Define a task definition with a custom job type and let the worker append result values into a parent-scoped variable after every inner flow completes
Define outputCollection with the list variable name and outputElement with an expression reading each inner flow's result variable to append on completion

a.text: Incorrect. Input mappings run on activation and create local variables for the ad-hoc sub-process; they do not run on each inner flow completion or build a result list.

b.text: Incorrect. The completionCondition is a boolean expression that decides when the ad-hoc sub-process is done; it is not the mechanism for collecting inner results into a list.

c.text: Incorrect. A job worker implementation gives the worker control, but the documented way to collect inner outputs is the outputCollection and outputElement pair, not custom worker writes.

d.text: Correct. outputCollection names the list variable and outputElement is evaluated on each inner flow completion to produce the value appended to that list; the list is propagated to the parent.

explanation: The ad-hoc sub-process appends each evaluated outputElement value into the outputCollection list as inner flows finish, and that local list is propagated to the parent scope when the sub-process completes.
